coverage.notes: Data.cs covered by DataTests (12 new Phase 2 tests). MemoryStack.cs covered by MemoryStackTests (5 new). Types/this.cs covered by EngineTypesTests (6 new KindOf tests). PLangContext.cs tested indirectly through MemoryStack context stamping tests. Methods.cs has no test — context stamping on handler result is untested.

findings.issue: Add() modifies _extensionToKind and _extensionToMime but does NOT update _allKinds or _mimeToKind. KindOf() uses _allKinds and _mimeToKind, so dynamically added extensions are invisible to KindOf(). Verified experimentally: after Add('.custom', 'custom-kind', 'application/custom'), KindOf('custom-kind') returns null. The existing Add test (Add_NewExtension_IsAccessible) only checks Kind() and Mime() — it does not check KindOf(), so the bug is untested.
findings.impact: Phase 2 wires Type.Kind through KindOf(). If a PLang developer uses types.Add() to register a custom extension, then creates Data with that kind, data.Type.Kind returns null. The Add → KindOf pipeline is broken. This will silently fail in Phase 4's envelope pipeline when custom types can't be wrapped.
findings.suggestion: Add() must also update _allKinds.Add(kind) and if mime is provided, _mimeToKind.TryAdd(mime, kind). Remove() must also clean up _allKinds (only if no other extension maps to the same kind) and _mimeToKind. Add test: after Add('.custom', 'custom-kind', 'application/custom'), verify KindOf('custom-kind') == 'custom-kind' and KindOf('application/custom') == 'custom-kind'.

findings.issue: Kind(null) and Mime(null) throw unhandled ArgumentNullException. Carried forward from v1 — not fixed. NormalizeExtension returns null for null input, then Dictionary.TryGetValue with OrdinalIgnoreCase comparer throws on null key.
findings.impact: Now more severe in Phase 2: Type.ClrType calls Engine.Types.Clr(Value) — if any Data has a null type Value, the null-coalescing chain would catch it. But direct Kind(null)/Mime(null) calls from file handlers or the pipeline still crash.
findings.suggestion: Add null guard in Kind() and Mime(): if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(extension)) return null / return 'application/octet-stream'.

findings.issue: Name() backtick issue — carried forward from v1. Now actively used by Data.Type lazy derivation (Data.cs line ~143: Types.Name(_value.GetType())). If a handler returns a HashSet<string> value, Data.Type.Value becomes 'hashset`1'.
findings.impact: Escalated from v1: was theoretical, now hits Data.Type derivation path. Users see backtick names in variable introspection.
findings.suggestion: Strip arity suffix: clrType.Name.Split('`')[0].ToLowerInvariant(). Add test for Name(typeof(HashSet<string>)).

findings.issue: BuilderNames() and ComplexSchemas() still have zero tests — carried forward from v1.
findings.impact: Unchanged from v1. Builder-facing API methods with no safety net.

findings.issue: Methods.cs stamps result.Context = context on handler result Data. This is the critical code path where action results enter the context system. No C# test covers this — it would require either integration testing or a PLang .goal test.
findings.impact: If this stamping line is accidentally removed or moved after the Return binding loop, return variables would get Data without context — Type.Kind, Type.Compressible, and lazy Type.ClrType via Engine.Types would all fail silently (fall back to static TypeMapping).
findings.suggestion: Add an integration test that runs an action handler, checks that the returned Data has context set. Or add a PLang .goal test that verifies a variable set by a handler has working Type.Kind.

findings.issue: DynamicData with no explicit type has broken Type derivation. DynamicData's constructor passes value=null to base Data, so base._value is null. Data.Type getter returns null when _value is null. But DynamicData.Value returns the factory result (non-null). So dov.Value is 42 but dov.Type is null.
findings.impact: Low — all DynamicData in practice (Now, NowUtc, GUID) have explicit types. But if a developer creates DynamicData without a type, the inconsistency between Value and Type is confusing.
findings.suggestion: Document that DynamicData requires explicit type. Or override Type getter to derive from factory value. Add test: DynamicData without explicit type — Type is null even though Value is non-null.

findings.issue: Type_LazyDerivation_WithContext test verifies Type.Value == 'string' and Type.ClrType == typeof(string). But it doesn't verify that the derivation went THROUGH Engine.Types (context path) rather than falling back to static TypeMapping. Both paths produce the same result for 'string'. A subtle bug in the context wiring would be masked.
findings.impact: If the context path is broken (e.g., _context is set but Engine.Types.Name is not called), the fallback to TypeMapping still produces 'string'. The test can't distinguish between the two paths.
findings.suggestion: Test with a type that Engine.Types handles differently from static TypeMapping (if any exist), or verify by adding a custom type via Engine.Types.Add() and checking that lazy derivation uses the custom mapping.

findings.issue: Phase 2 changes how Data derives its type at runtime (lazy, context-based). No PLang .goal test validates that the full pipeline (builder → .pr → runtime) produces Data with correct context and Type derivation.
findings.impact: The C# tests verify individual components. But a PLang test would catch integration issues — e.g., the builder generates a .pr file with a type that Engine.Types doesn't recognize, or GoalMapper doesn't stamp context correctly.
findings.suggestion: Create a PLang .goal test: set a variable, read it back, verify its type matches expectations. This validates the full pipeline.

summary: 1324 tests pass, 0 failures. Phase 2 tests are well-structured for the context propagation chain (PLangContext → MemoryStack → Data → Type → Kind/Compressible). New KindOf tests are solid. However: (1) CRITICAL: Add() doesn't update _allKinds/_mimeToKind, breaking KindOf() for dynamically added extensions — verified experimentally; (2) v1 null crash and backtick findings carry forward unfixed, now with higher impact since Phase 2 actively uses the affected code paths; (3) Methods.cs context stamping has no test; (4) BuilderNames/ComplexSchemas still untested. Verdict: needs-fixes — finding #1 is a real bug, not just a test gap.
